hero.subtitle: The Trial and Execution of Nine Ogoni Activists, 1995
hero.description: A documentation of the 1995 military tribunal and execution of Ken Saro-Wiwa and eight Ogoni activists, and the censored trial speech that was never permitted in court.

courtroom.heading: The Tribunal Room
courtroom.body: Four recordings document the 1995 Special Military Tribunal from distinct positions — each reflecting how location in the room shaped one's encounter with the proceedings.
courtroom.footnote: Recordings will be linked as they become available.
courtroom.heroFootnote: Recordings will be linked as they become available.

perspectives.label: Judge's Bench
perspectives.description: From the military tribunal judge's position. The accused stand before you. The weight of the state's authority is at your back.

perspectives.label: Front Row
perspectives.description: Behind the accused. You are close enough to see their faces. The tribunal's words land heavy in the air between you.

perspectives.label: Back of Room
perspectives.description: From the rear of the gallery. The accused are small figures at the front. The room feels vast. Sound carries differently here.

perspectives.label: Side Gallery
perspectives.description: Where international observers and press were permitted to sit, under restriction. A different angle on power.
